_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: TX House HD114-118 (Dallas + San Antonio/Bexar delegation, state tier). Four Democrats (John Bryant HD114, Cassandra Garcia Hernandez HD115, Trey Martinez Fischer HD116, Philip Cortez HD117) + one swing-district Republican (John Lujan HD118). Evidence from Texas Right to Life / Texas Alliance for Life / Texas Values Action scorecards, Choice Tracker roll calls, John Birch Society Freedom Index roll-call records, Texans for Fiscal Responsibility fiscal index, the 2021 SB1 Democratic quorum break, the SB2 (2025) voucher tally, candidate sites/questionnaires, and group endorsements. DEVIATION FLAGS: (1) Philip Cortez (D, HD117) is a documented cross-party outlier — voted YES on HB796 Texas Sovereignty Act, YES on HB9 border-security funds, and (per Freedom Index, single-source) YES on HB1927 permitless carry; also YES on HB1056 sound-money. (2) John Lujan (R, HD118) is socially conservative (TAL 100%, TVA 86%) but deviated LEFT by voting YES on HB1738 to repeal the homosexual-conduct crime (confirmed by Texas Values Action + Freedom Index), and deviated RIGHT-of-norm fiscally (Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade F). CURRENCY: Lujan is NOT seeking HD-118 re-election in 2026 — he ran for U.S. House TX-35 and LOST the May 2026 Republican runoff; he still holds HD-118 through Jan 2027 (candidacy_status set not_running). Cells with only party-inference, no individually-cited record, or a bill-text/cell mismatch left null. The HB1056 sound-money votes (Bryant, Cortez, Lujan) are noted but NOT scored, since the economic q0 cell is specifically CBDC/crypto, not precious-metals legal tender.

profile.religion: Lifelong SMU figure (B.A. 1969, J.D. 1972); as of 2023 pursuing a Spiritual Discipline degree at SMU's United Methodist Perkins School of Theology (only documented faith data point).
profile.background: TX House District 114 (Dallas Co.), Democrat, since Jan 2023. John Wiley Bryant: former U.S. Representative for TX-5 (1983-1997) and earlier TX House member (1974-1983); attorney. Sits on House Public Education and Pensions/Investments/Financial Services. Described as 'the Muscle of Texas' Progressive Caucus.'

john-bryant@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-114 (D), since Jan 2023; veteran former U.S. Congressman. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Consistent progressive (Freedom Index 8%, Texas Alliance for Life 45%): self-described 'pro-choice champion' who has 'always fought to preserve Roe'; voted YES on HB1738 (2025 repeal of the homosexual-conduct crime) and publicly opposed SB14 (2023 trans-youth ban); on House Public Education, called SB2 (2025 voucher) 'a catastrophe' and voted NO; floor-opposed SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms) on Establishment grounds; wants SB1-era voting restrictions repealed and voting made easier; voted NO on HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act); opposed border-barrier spending; explicitly anti-NRA, supports an assault-weapons ban and restoring gun-permit requirements. CROSS-PARTY NOTE (not scored): per Freedom Index he voted YES on HB1056 'Constitutional Money' (gold/silver legal tender) — a sound-money vote, but the economic q0 cell is CBDC/crypto-specific, so left null pending bill-text confirmation. Confirmed running for re-election (won the March 2026 D primary; faces R Tim McDonough in November). Life/marriage roll calls predating his 2023 return (SB8 2021, etc.) and public-justice positions returned no individual evidence and are left null.

0.note: Self-described 'pro-choice champion' who has 'always fought to preserve Roe v. Wade' and pledges to 'restore a woman's right to make her own choice' — contrary to personhood-at-conception.

1.note: Pledges to sponsor legislation restoring abortion access; opposite of abortion abolition.

2.note: Publicly opposed SB14 (2023 youth gender-transition ban) before the House vote — rejects codifying biological-sex limits on care.

4.note: Voted YES on HB1738 (2025) to repeal the crime of homosexual conduct — pro-LGBTQ position.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher), calling it 'a catastrophe'; sits on House Public Education and argues funds should go to public schools.

3.note: Floor-opposed SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms), arguing it unfairly forces non-Christian/Jewish taxpayers to subsidize a Christian display.

1.note: Says SB1-era voter restrictions 'should be repealed' and wants voting 'easy and accessible' — opposite of the photo-ID/citizenship-verification posture.

3.note: Criticizes SB1 for making it 'harder to vote'; supports expanded mail/ballot access — contrary to opposing mass mail-in voting and drop boxes.

0.note: Voted NO on HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act) and NO on HJR98 (Article V Convention) — the anti-nullification side.
Criticized border-barrier spending, arguing the money could build rural hospitals instead — opposes a completed physical barrier.
Vows to 'restore gun permit requirements' — contrary to permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: 'Not afraid of the NRA gun lobby'; will fight for 'banning sale of military assault rifles' and 'reasonable gun restrictions' — favors an AWB and permit mandates.

profile.religion: No explicit faith statement found; Wikipedia notes service on boards affiliated with Catholic charities (indirect).
profile.background: TX House District 115 (Dallas Co. - Coppell/Irving/Carrollton/Farmers Branch), Democrat, since Jan 2025. Formal legislative name is Cassandra GARCIA Hernandez. Immigration/trial attorney; former Dallas Ethics Commissioner; domestic-violence survivor. On House Appropriations and Intergovernmental Affairs (Vice Chair, State-Federal Relations subcmte); MALC Freshman of the Year. Succeeded Julie Johnson (left for U.S. House).

cassandra-hernandez@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-115 (D), since Jan 2025; formal name Cassandra GARCIA Hernandez. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Mainstream progressive freshman: Planned Parenthood Texas Votes-endorsed (2024) and rated 100% pro-choice; featured in the Avow Texas voter guide pledging to 'restore' abortion access; an Everytown for Gun Safety Fellow who opposes permitless carry and campus carry and backs handgun permits/safe-storage; pro-public-school and anti-voucher ('fully fund public schools'); an immigration attorney who campaigns against bills that 'target immigrants'; supports expanded mail-voting access. Confirmed running for re-election (advanced from the March 2026 D primary; faces R Danny Rosellini in November). NOTE: her Texas Alliance for Life 2025 score is 45% (vs an expected ~0%), reflecting yes-votes on near-consensus non-abortion bills (bereavement/Life-of-the-Mother), not a pro-life shift. Her 31 authored bills were not individually retrievable this pass; Christian-liberty, fiscal, and federal-overreach positions returned no individual evidence and are left null.

0.note: Planned Parenthood-rated 100% pro-choice and Avow-featured; pledges she 'won't yield on women's constitutional right to choose' — contrary to personhood-at-conception.

1.note: Pledged to 'roll back the bans' and opposes SB8 — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (2024) — aligned with the abortion industry.
Campaigns on having 'fought bills that target the LGBTQ+ community' — promotes/protects LGBTQ identity in public policy.

0.note: Pledges to 'fully fund public schools with state funds' and opposes 'undermining public schools' — contrary to universal school choice/vouchers.

3.note: Supports expanded mail-voting access and rules limiting poll-watcher authority — contrary to opposing mass mail-in voting.

1.note: Immigration attorney who campaigns against bills that 'target immigrants' — contrary to mandatory deportation.

2.note: Pro-immigrant advocacy opposing anti-immigrant legislation — contrary to opposing sanctuary policies.

0.note: Opposes permitless carry and campus open carry; advocates 'permits for loaded handguns' — contrary to constitutional carry.

1.note: An Everytown for Gun Safety Fellow backing safe-storage mandates and a DV-offender database — supports new firearm restrictions.

profile.religion: Catholic — raised in a Catholic family, served as an altar boy at St. Luke Catholic Church, San Antonio.
profile.background: TX House District 116 (San Antonio/Bexar Co.), Democrat, since 2019 (also held the seat 2001-2017). Former Texas House Democratic Caucus Chair / Minority Leader (2023-2025) and former chair of the Mexican American Legislative Caucus; Vice-Chair of House Ways & Means; 10th in House seniority. Attorney.

trey-martinez-fischer@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-116 (D), since 2019 (prior 2001-2017); former House Democratic Caucus Chair/Minority Leader. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Consistent, high-profile progressive: Avow Texas and Planned Parenthood rate him at/near 100% on abortion; Texans for Fiscal Responsibility scores him 26/100 (historical 'F'); self-stated NRA 'D' rating, opposes open/campus/permitless carry ('Guns and education do not mix'); voted NO on SB2 (2025 voucher); did NOT join the three Democrats who crossed to support SB14 (2023 trans-youth ban). He ORGANIZED/LED the July 2021 Democratic quorum break to Washington that blocked SB1 (the election-integrity bill), and led the 2025 redistricting walkout to Illinois. Confirmed running for re-election in 2026 (declined a DA run; faces R Rhett Rosenquest Smith). No cross-party conservative deviations found. Individual roll calls on Christian-liberty bills (SB10/SB11), bail (SB6), and several life bills were not separately isolated this pass and are left null; his caucus-bloc position is documented but the per-bill citation was not pulled.

0.note: Avow Texas 100% rating and 'Planned Parenthood Champion'; publicly calls the abortion bans 'draconian' — contrary to personhood-at-conception.

1.note: 100% pro-abortion-rights rating; opposes the bans — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Avow-endorsed 'Planned Parenthood Champion' — aligned with the abortion industry.

2.note: Voted against SB14 (2023 youth gender-transition ban); not among the three Democratic crossovers (Dutton, King, Thierry).

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher), warning vouchers would outspend public schools — contrary to universal school choice.

1.note: Organized/led the July 2021 Democratic quorum break to D.C. to block SB1 (photo voter-ID / citizenship-verification provisions).

3.note: The quorum break specifically opposed SB1's mail-in restrictions, drop-box ban, and anti-harvesting rules.

0.note: Self-stated NRA 'D' rating; opposes open and campus carry ('Guns and education do not mix') — contrary to permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Backs expanded background checks and gun-control measures — contrary to opposing new firearm restrictions.

profile.background: TX House District 117 (western San Antonio/Bexar Co.), Democrat; elected 2012, lost 2014, regained 2016, re-elected since. U.S. Air Force Reserve captain; former San Antonio City Council member (2007-2011); PhD in Educational Administration (UT Austin). On Homeland Security/Public Safety/Veterans' Affairs and Intergovernmental Affairs. Considered a business-friendly moderate.

philip-cortez@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-117 (D), multiple terms since 2012; Air Force Reserve captain. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Progressive on the marquee social issues but a documented CROSS-PARTY OUTLIER on several others: voted NO on SB8 (2021 heartbeat), HB1280 (trigger ban), and SB4 (Choice Tracker), is PP Texas Votes-endorsed, voted YES on HB1738 (2023 repeal of the homosexual-conduct crime), voted NO on SB2 (2025 voucher), and opposed SB1 strenuously enough to break quorum in 2021 (though he was the first Democrat to return to negotiate, and declined to break quorum at all in 2025). Conservative groups rate him low (Freedom Index 13%, Texans for Fiscal Responsibility 'F'). DEVIATIONS (broke RIGHT of the D line, per Freedom Index roll-call records): YES on HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act / 10th-Amendment anti-commandeering), YES on HB9 (2023 border-security funds / Operation Lone Star), and YES on HB1927 (2021 permitless carry); plus YES on HB1056 sound-money and SB1070 (ERIC withdrawal). CAUTION (transparency): the HB1927 vote comes from a single secondary source (Freedom Index) and could not be re-confirmed against the TLO roll call this pass; the HB9 border vote is scored as documented support for active border presence. Texas Alliance for Life rates him 59% (consensus maternal-health bills, not a pro-life shift). Do NOT conflate the Freedom-Index '2023 SB8 - Yes' entry with the 2021 SB8 ban, which he voted NO on.

0.note: Voted NO on SB8 (2021 heartbeat), HB1280 (trigger ban), and SB4; PP Texas Votes-endorsed — contrary to personhood-at-conception.

1.note: Voted NO on the Human Life Protection trigger ban (HB1280) — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (2022 & 2024) — aligned with the abortion industry.
Voted YES on HB1738 (2023) to repeal the crime of homosexual conduct — pro-LGBTQ position.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher) and the voucher-ballot amendment, YES on HB2 school funding; longstanding anti-voucher record — contrary to universal school choice.

1.note: Broke quorum in 2021 to block SB1 (photo voter-ID provisions), though he was the first Democrat to return to negotiate.

3.note: The quorum break opposed SB1's mail-in restrictions, drop-box ban, and anti-harvesting rules.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YES on HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act), a 10th-Amendment anti-commandeering measure — and NO on HJR98 (Article V Convention).
CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YES on HB9 (2023) border-security funding (Operation Lone Star), supporting active border presence.

CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: per the Freedom Index roll-call record he voted YES on HB1927 (2021 permitless/constitutional carry). CAUTION: single-source; not re-confirmed against the TLO roll call this pass.

profile.religion: Baptist — active member of Southside Baptist Church, San Antonio, where he teaches Sunday School.
profile.background: TX House District 118 (south/southeast San Antonio/Bexar Co.), Republican; won 2016 special, 2021 special runoff, re-elected 2022 & 2024 in a genuine swing seat (73% Hispanic, Biden +14 in 2020). 25-year San Antonio firefighter and former Bexar County sheriff's deputy; co-founder of an IT firm. NOT seeking HD-118 re-election in 2026 — ran for U.S. House TX-35 and lost the May 2026 GOP runoff; holds HD-118 through Jan 2027.

john-lujan@TX.notes_set: TX State Representative, HD-118 (R), since the 2021 special; Baptist Sunday-school teacher and career first-responder in a swing district. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] CURRENCY: NOT running for HD-118 re-election in 2026 — he sought U.S. House TX-35 and LOST the May 2026 Republican runoff (candidacy_status set not_running; still the sitting member through Jan 2027). Solidly socially conservative: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025), Texas Right to Life 86%, Texas Values Action 86%; voted YES on SB8/SB33 (life), HB229 'What is a Woman Act' and SB1257 (biological sex), SB2 (2025 voucher — the key swing-R vote, and he opposed the 2023 amendment that stripped ESA language), SB12 (Parents' Bill of Rights), SB17 (DEI ban), SB10 (Ten Commandments) and SB11 (school prayer), and HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act); supported Operation Lone Star/border-wall funding; NRA-PVF, TSRA, and CLEAT (police union) endorsed. DEVIATIONS: (LEFT) voted YES on HB1738 (2025) to repeal the homosexual-conduct crime — confirmed by Texas Values Action and the Freedom Index, and Equality Texas grades him 'F'; (RIGHT-of-norm fiscally) Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade 'F' (index 57), dinged for voting against property-tax relief (HB2) and spending restraint, and he voted NO on HJR98 (Article V Convention). NO HB1927 vote possible — he was not seated until Nov 2021, after permitless carry was signed (left null). Election-specific cells left null (SB1070 ERIC withdrawal doesn't map cleanly to a rubric cell; not seated for SB1 2021).

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 100% (2025), Texas Right to Life 86%; voted YES on SB8 (Women's Privacy Act) and SB33 (no taxpayer abortion funding).

4.note: Consistent pro-life record (TAL 100%); no abortion-industry funding documented.

2.note: Voted YES on HB229 (2025 'What is a Woman Act' / binary biological-sex definitions) and SB1257 (Detransitioners' Bill of Rights).

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher) and against the 2023 Herrero amendment that stripped ESA language — consistently pro-school-choice.

1.note: Voted YES on SB12 (2025 Parents' Bill of Rights), strengthening parental notification/consent.

2.note: Voted YES on SB17 (2023 DEI ban) — opposes CRT/SOGI-type frameworks in public institutions.

3.note: Voted YES on SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms) and SB11 (prayer/Bible-reading time in schools).

2.note: RIGHT-of-norm DEVIATION: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade 'F' (index 57) — dinged for voting against property-tax relief (HB2) and against rainy-day-fund/spending restraint; rated a fiscal moderate.

0.note: Career first-responder (25-yr firefighter, former Bexar County sheriff's deputy) endorsed by CLEAT (the police union) — affirmative pro-law-enforcement profile.
Voted YES on HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act), a 10th-Amendment anti-commandeering measure. (NOTE: voted NO on HJR98 Article V Convention — a right-flank deviation.)
Supported Operation Lone Star and border-wall construction funding; opposed scaling it back — backs active border presence.

1.note: NRA-PVF and Texas State Rifle Association endorsed; voted YES on HB2837 (2023) blocking firearm-merchant credit-card codes (anti-backdoor-registry) — opposes new gun restrictions/registries.
